title: NVIDIA LLM Profile v1
description: Vendor profile for scope_type='llm' that captures the canonical OpenAI Chat Completions request and response metadata. Extends ATOF default/llm.v1 (which carries only model_name) with sampling parameters, response identity, finish state, and full token-usage breakdown. All fields are optional — emit only what your runtime knows. Extension of: default/llm.v1 (atof-event-format.md §6.1). Schema ID format: vendor/<id>.v<N> (atof-event-format.md §4.2).

model_name.description: Inherited from default/llm.v1. The model identifier sent to the provider — e.g., 'gpt-4.1', 'nvidia/nemotron-3-super-v3', 'meta/llama-3.1-70b-instruct'.

provider.description: Provider namespace — 'openai', 'anthropic', 'nvidia', 'azure-openai', 'bedrock', 'litellm', etc. Used by consumers to dispatch on provider-specific quirks.

completion_id.description: Provider-assigned response identifier. For OpenAI Chat Completions this is the response 'id' field (e.g., 'chatcmpl-abc123'). Used for log correlation and provider-side audit lookups.

system_fingerprint.description: Provider-assigned model deployment fingerprint (OpenAI 'system_fingerprint'). Identifies the backend configuration; changes between deployments even when 'model_name' is stable.

service_tier.description: Service tier requested or used — e.g., 'auto', 'default', 'flex', 'scale', 'priority'. May differ between request and response when 'auto' resolves to a concrete tier.

request.description: Sampling and dispatch parameters sent on the request. Mirrors OpenAI Chat Completions request fields that affect generation behavior. Omit any field the runtime did not set explicitly.

n.description: Number of completions requested. ATOF emits one event per scope; if n>1, the additional choices live in 'output.choices'.

max_completion_tokens.description: Modern max-completion-tokens cap (replaces 'max_tokens' for OpenAI o-series).

parallel_tool_calls.description: Whether parallel tool calls are permitted within a single completion.

reasoning_effort.description: Reasoning effort (OpenAI o-series and equivalent reasoning models).

stream.description: Whether the request was streamed. When true, expect a corresponding 'streaming' flag on the ScopeStart event.

user.description: End-user identifier passed to the provider for abuse monitoring.

finish_reason.description: Why generation stopped. Sourced from the FIRST choice (choices[0].finish_reason) when n > 1; per-choice values live in 'output.choices'.

usage.description: Token-usage and cost breakdown. Mirrors OpenAI Chat Completions 'usage' with nested details. All counts are optional — emit only what the provider returned. Maps directly to ATIF Step.metrics fields.

prompt_tokens.description: Input tokens including cached and non-cached.

total_tokens.description: prompt_tokens + completion_tokens (provider may compute differently — preserve as reported).

cached_tokens.description: Subset of prompt_tokens served from cache.

audio_tokens.description: Subset of prompt_tokens that were audio inputs.

reasoning_tokens.description: Internal-reasoning tokens not included in the visible response (OpenAI o-series).

audio_tokens.description: Subset of completion_tokens emitted as audio.

cost_usd.description: Cost in USD computed by the runtime (not provider-reported).

logprobs.description: Log-probability information when 'logprobs: true' was requested. Shape mirrors OpenAI Chat Completions 'choices[0].logprobs'. Per-choice logprobs for n > 1 belong in 'output.choices'.

content.description: Per-token logprob entries with optional top_logprobs.

refusal.description: Per-token logprobs for a refusal completion.
Refusal text when the provider declined to answer. Distinct from 'finish_reason' = 'content_filter' (content-policy block) — refusal is the model's own safety response.

request_metadata.description: Provider-passthrough metadata (OpenAI 'metadata' request field) — small key/value strings the runtime attached for observability.

latency_ms.description: End-to-end latency from request send to first stop, in milliseconds. Convenience surface — derivable from ATOF Start/End ts_micros, exposed here for consumers that read profiles standalone.

ttft_ms.description: Time-to-first-token, in milliseconds. Only meaningful when streamed.
